cards.notes: Universal greeting, used in all contexts.
Used until noon.
Used from noon until evening.
Used as greeting in the evening or to say goodbye at night.
Essential politeness word. Literally: 'as a favor.'
Muchas gracias = thank you very much.
Literally: 'of nothing.' Alternative: No hay de qué.
Perdón for bumping into someone. Disculpe to get attention politely.
Very useful phrase for beginners. Entender = to understand.
Formal 'usted' form. Informal: ¿Hablas inglés?
Essential for shopping. Plural: ¿Cuánto cuestan?
El baño = bathroom. El servicio = restroom (more formal).
Polite way to order in restaurants or make requests.
Used in restaurants. La cuenta = the bill/check.
Feminine noun but uses 'el' article (el agua fría). To avoid 'la a-' sound clash.
Tomar is more common in Latin America for 'to drink.'
A la izquierda = to the left. Siga derecho = go straight.
Mañana also means 'morning' (la mañana). Context determines meaning.
Ayuda = help (noun). Ayudar = to help (verb).
Literally: 'I call myself...' ¿Cómo te llamas? = What's your name? (informal)
